data.title: Bombardier delays 2025 forecast because of tariff doubts
data.description:
Bombardier (BBD.B:CA) on Thursday said it didn’t provide an earnings forecast for the current year because of the uncertain effects of a trade dispute between Canada and the United States.

The Canadian planemaker also beat Wall Street’s estimates for quarterly earnings amid sustained demand for jets.

The 25% tariffs on Canadian and Mexican goods that U.S. President Donald Trump last week was set to impose have clouded the outlook for companies such as Bombardier (BBD.B:CA). The countries agreed to delay tariff actions for 30 days as they negotiate issues including border security.

Most of the jets that Bombardier (BBD.B:CA) assembles in Canada are sold to U.S. customers. Before the tariff threat, Bombardier (BBD.B:CA) and other aircraft makers faced supply-chain constraints including shortages in parts and skilled labor amid surging demand for air travel as the Covid-19 pandemic ebbed.

Quarterly earnings beat

Bombardier’s (BBD.B:CA) revenue rose 1.5% from a year earlier to $3.11 billion in the fourth quarter, compared with the average estimate of $3.18 billion among analysts.

Earning adjusted for one-time items were $3.01 a share, beating the consensus estimate of $2.03.

Yearly results

Full-year revenue rose 8% from the prior year to $8.67 billion, and its services business generated $2.04 billion in yearly revenue, hitting management’s target a year earlier than expected.

Bombardier (BBD.B:CA) last year delivered 146 aircraft, up from 138 in 2023, but shy of its November forecast of 150-155 jets.

Its free cash flow was $232 million, in line with targets.

The planemaker's order backlog edged up by 1% from $14.2 billion to $14.4 billion in 2024.

data.title: Beazer Homes to quicken pace of share repurchases
data.description:
• Beazer Homes USA (NYSE:BZH) said it has quickened the pace of its share repurchases amid the share price dislocation.
  • The firm said it will continue to buyback shares after its previously authorized share repurchase program, under which ~$24.8 million remained available.
  • The company also said it plans to up the pace of its repurchases in light of current market conditions.
  • The firm also said its debt reduction was expected to moderate in the near term.
  • BZH now expects its net debt to net capitalization ratio to be in the low 30% range by the end of fiscal 2026 and will be in the mid-to-high 30% range by the end of fiscal 2025.

data.title: Wave Life Sciences initiates early stage clinical trial of WVE-007 in obesity
data.description:
• Wave Life Sciences (NASDAQ:WVE) announced clinical trial application approval and initiation of the Phase 1 INLIGHT clinical trial of WVE-007 in obesity.
  • Wave expects to deliver proof-of-concept clinical data in 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Thursday: HON, AMZN and more
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures on Thursday were slightly higher on Thursday, as market participants continued to wade through an earnings deluge. Here are some stocks to watch on Thursday:

  • Shares of Eli Lilly (LLY) fell slightly in pre-market trading. The world's largest pharmaceutical firm delivered a 45% Y/Y increase in Q4 revenue. The sales jump was powered by the continued blockbuster success of tirzepatide, marketed by Lilly (LLY) as diabetes treatment Mounjaro and weight-loss therapy Zepbound. The former drug brought in revenue of $3.53B in the quarter, a 60% surge. Meanwhile, the latter medicine brought in $1.91B in revenue. The Indianapolis, Ind.-based firm also provided 2025 guidance that was above expectations. See how LLY performed in charts.
  • Honeywell (HON) stock slipped about 3% ahead of the opening bell, after the company forecasted disappointing top- and bottom-line figures for 2025. However, the big news was not HON's results, but its announcement that it would separate into three separately listed firms, ending one of the last few U.S. industrial conglomerates. The move comes after pressure from activist investor Elliott. "Our simplification of Honeywell has rapidly advanced over the past year, and we will continue to shape our portfolio to create further shareholder value," top boss Vimal Kapur said.
  • Shares of Amazon (AMZN) will be in focus, ahead of the e-commerce and tech giant's Q4 results after the closing bell. Wall Street expects the Seattle, Wash.-based firm to earn $1.48 per share on revenue of $187.23B. The latter figure is seen rising 10.1% Y/Y, as its e-commerce arm gets a boost from a strong U.S. holiday season and its cloud services gets a lift from more adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) processes. Reuters on Wednesday reported that Amazon (AMZN) was set to release a version of its popular voice service Alexa with generative AI.
  • Take-Two Interactive Software (TTWO) stock will also garner attention, as the video game publisher gears up for its FQ3 2025 results after markets close. Wall Street expects the company to earn 56 cents per share on revenue of $1.39B. Investors will be closely watching for any updates or commentary on its long-awaited Grand Theft Auto VI game. Last week, Epyllion CEO Matthew Ball said some video game makers were hopeful that Take-Two (TTWO) will leverage the unprecedented excitement around the game to price it up to $100.
